headline: "Attention Is All You Need" paper published
summary: Google Brain and Google Research publish the Transformer architecture paper, foundational to modern LLMs.

headline: OECD AI Principles adopted
summary: OECD member countries adopt principles for trustworthy AI.

headline: OpenAI releases GPT-3
summary: OpenAI releases GPT-3 with 175 billion parameters, demonstrating strong few-shot learning.

headline: EU AI Act proposed
summary: European Commission publishes its proposal for a regulation on artificial intelligence.

headline: China AI algorithm regulation effective
summary: China's regulation on deep synthesis (deepfakes) and algorithmic recommendation comes into force.

headline: OpenAI launches ChatGPT
summary: OpenAI launches ChatGPT, bringing LLM interaction to the general public.

headline: NIST AI Risk Management Framework published
summary: The US National Institute of Standards and Technology publishes the AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), providing voluntary guidance for managing risks in AI systems.

headline: OpenAI releases GPT-4
summary: OpenAI releases GPT-4, a multimodal large language model.

headline: Anthropic releases Claude 1
summary: Anthropic releases Claude 1, its first publicly available large language model.

headline: UK publishes pro-innovation AI regulation white paper
summary: The UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology publishes its white paper setting out a principles-based, pro-innovation approach to AI regulation — distinct from the EU's prescriptive risk-based model.

headline: "Pause Giant AI Experiments" open letter
summary: Future of Life Institute publishes open letter signed by thousands — including Yoshua Bengio, Stuart Russell, and Elon Musk — calling for a six-month pause on training AI systems more powerful than GPT-4.

headline: Meta releases Llama 2
summary: Meta releases Llama 2, an open-weight large language model family available for commercial use.

headline: China Generative AI regulation effective
summary: China's Interim Measures for the Management of Generative AI Services take effect.

headline: Switzerland FADP fully effective
summary: Switzerland's revised Federal Act on Data Protection (nFADP) is fully in effect, with relevance to AI data processing.

headline: Microsoft integrates Copilot into Windows 11
summary: Microsoft embeds Copilot as a system-level AI assistant in Windows 11, accessible via the taskbar — AI moves from standalone apps into the operating system.

headline: G7 Hiroshima AI Process Code of Conduct adopted
summary: The G7 nations and the EU agree to the Hiroshima Process International Code of Conduct for organisations developing advanced AI systems, providing voluntary guidance on risk assessment, transparency, safety research, and systemic risk management.

headline: US Executive Order on AI
summary: President Biden signs Executive Order 14110 on safe, secure, and trustworthy AI.

headline: Google DeepMind releases Gemini 1.0
summary: Google DeepMind releases Gemini, a natively multimodal model family.

headline: ISO 42001:2023 published
summary: The International Organization for Standardization publishes ISO/IEC 42001:2023, the first international standard for AI management systems, providing a framework for responsible development and use of AI.

headline: Anthropic releases Claude 3
summary: Anthropic releases the Claude 3 model family (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus).

headline: UN General Assembly adopts first AI resolution
summary: The UN General Assembly unanimously adopts its first resolution on AI, calling for safe, secure and trustworthy AI systems for sustainable development and urging member states to develop regulatory and governance frameworks.

headline: EU AI Act adopted by Parliament
summary: European Parliament votes to adopt the EU AI Act.

headline: Meta releases Llama 3
summary: Meta releases Llama 3 in 8B and 70B parameter variants.

headline: OpenAI releases GPT-4o
summary: OpenAI releases GPT-4o ("omni"), a natively multimodal model with voice, vision, and text.

headline: EU AI Act enters into force
summary: The EU AI Act is published in the Official Journal and enters into force.

headline: Council of Europe AI Convention opens for signature
summary: The Council of Europe opens the Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence and Human Rights, Democracy and the Rule of Law (CETS No. 225) for signature in Vilnius — the first binding international treaty on AI, open to both Council of Europe members and non-member states including the US, UK, and Canada.

headline: OpenAI releases o1-preview (thinking model)
summary: OpenAI releases o1-preview, a model trained with reinforcement learning to perform complex reasoning.

headline: Anthropic launches Claude computer use
summary: Anthropic releases computer use in public beta — Claude can control a computer by viewing the screen, moving a cursor, clicking buttons, and typing text. A stepping stone toward autonomous agents.

headline: Apple integrates ChatGPT into iOS and macOS
summary: Apple ships iOS 18.2 and macOS 15.2 with ChatGPT integration, allowing Siri to hand off queries to OpenAI's model and enabling ChatGPT in system-wide Writing Tools.

headline: OpenAI releases o3 (reasoning model)
summary: OpenAI announces o3, a next-generation reasoning model, initially available to researchers.

headline: DeepSeek releases DeepSeek-R1
summary: DeepSeek releases R1, an open-weight reasoning model competitive with leading Western models.

headline: OpenAI releases Operator (autonomous agent)
summary: OpenAI releases Operator, an AI agent that can browse the web and perform tasks autonomously — marking the shift from conversational AI to agentic AI.

headline: EU AI Act — prohibited practices apply
summary: The EU AI Act's provisions on prohibited AI practices and AI literacy obligations become applicable.

headline: Switzerland signs Council of Europe AI Convention
summary: Switzerland signs the Council of Europe's Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence and Human Rights, Democracy and the Rule of Law (CETS No. 225), the first binding international treaty on AI.

headline: Meta releases Llama 4
summary: Meta releases Llama 4 Scout and Maverick, the first open-weight natively multimodal MoE models.

headline: Anthropic releases Claude 4
summary: Anthropic releases Claude 4 Opus and Claude 4 Sonnet with major capability gains.

headline: Japan AI Promotion Act effective
summary: Japan's Act on the Promotion of Research and Development and Utilisation of AI-Related Technologies takes effect.

headline: EU AI Act — GPAI model rules apply
summary: Rules for general-purpose AI models and governance obligations under the EU AI Act become applicable.

headline: OpenAI releases GPT-5
summary: OpenAI releases GPT-5, a major leap in intelligence across coding, math, writing, and vision.

headline: Mistral AI releases Mistral 3 family
summary: Mistral AI releases the Mistral 3 family: Large 3 frontier model plus nine dense open-weight models.

headline: South Korea Framework Act on AI in force
summary: South Korea's Framework Act on the Development of Artificial Intelligence and the Creation of a Foundation for Trust enters into force, establishing a national governance framework for AI.

headline: US National Policy Framework for AI published
summary: The White House publishes the National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence, setting out legislative recommendations for AI governance in the United States.

headline: Anthropic releases Claude Mythos Preview
summary: Anthropic announces Claude Mythos Preview, a frontier model with advanced cybersecurity capabilities, through Project Glasswing — a coordinated partnership with AWS, Apple, Google, Microsoft, and others. The model excels at identifying and exploiting software vulnerabilities but is restricted to vetted organizations for defensive security work due to its offensive potential.

headline: Anthropic releases Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
summary: Anthropic releases Claude Fable 5 — which it describes as its most capable generally available model and its first Mythos-class model offered for broad public use — alongside Claude Mythos 5, the same underlying model with cybersecurity safeguards lifted, available to a small group of vetted cyberdefenders through Project Glasswing. Mythos 5 succeeds the April 2026 Claude Mythos Preview. Anthropic says Fable 5 redirects requests touching cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry to Claude Opus 4.8, and reports these safeguards trigger in under 5% of sessions.

headline: EU AI Act omnibus agreement proposed — postpones high-risk deadlines
summary: European Commission proposes omnibus amendment to the EU AI Act. Postponements: Annex III standalone high-risk systems from August 2, 2026 to December 2, 2027; Annex I safety components from August 2, 2027 to August 2, 2028; regulatory sandboxes from August 2, 2026 to August 2, 2027.

headline: EU AI Act — watermarking obligations apply
summary: Watermarking obligations for generative AI systems become applicable (Art. 50). A grace period extends compliance to December 2, 2026.

headline: EU AI Act — prohibited practices deadline
summary: Providers of AI systems for generating intimate images and deepfake video/audio must comply with the prohibition on non-consensual intimate content (Art. 5).

headline: EU AI Act — regulatory sandboxes deadline (postponed)
summary: Each Member State must establish at least one regulatory sandbox for AI testing (originally August 2, 2026, postponed via EU omnibus agreement). Commission clarifies sectoral exclusions (Art. 2(2)) and publishes guidance on transparency for AI-generated content.

headline: EU AI Act — post-market monitoring guidance
summary: Commission publishes guidance and template for post-market monitoring of AI systems.

headline: EU AI Act — Annex III high-risk systems deadline (postponed)
summary: Standalone high-risk AI systems listed in Annex III must achieve full compliance with all EU AI Act obligations (Chapter III). Originally scheduled for August 2, 2026, postponed via EU omnibus agreement.

headline: EU AI Act — safety components in regulated products deadline (postponed)
summary: AI systems embedded as safety components in products regulated under sectoral harmonisation legislation must achieve full compliance (Annex I, Chapter III). Originally scheduled for August 2, 2027, postponed via EU omnibus agreement.
